title: Roasted Rangy Malaysian Prawns Pasta
description: A peppery, snappy Malaysian dish made with linguine pasta, featuring agemono-deep-fried prawns as a hangover cure. This sugar-free-friendly recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Cook the linguine pasta according to the package instructions until al dente.
Meanwhile, peel and de-vein the prawns and set aside.
Heat the coconut oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the peanut oil and swirl it around to coat the pan.
Add the garlic and ginger and sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the chili flakes and paprika and sauté for another minute.
Add the prawns and toss them to coat with the spices.
Deep-fry the prawns until golden brown and crispy, about 2-3 minutes.
Remove the prawns from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate.
Add the fish sauce and palm sugar to the pan and stir to combine.
Add the cooked linguine pasta to the pan and toss it with the sauce.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the pasta hot, topped with the deep-fried prawns.
